org.key_project.util.java

Class ArrayUtil

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.key_project.util.java.ArrayUtil

  • public final class ArrayUtil
extends Object
    Provides static methods to work with arrays.
    Author:
    Martin Hentschel

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Modifier Constructor and Description
      private ArrayUtil()
      Forbid instances by this private constructor.

      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static int[] add(int[] array, int toAdd)
      Adds the given element to the existing array.

      Adds the given element to the existing array.

      Adds the given elements to the existing array.

      Adds the given elements to the existing array.

      Checks if the given array contains the element to search.

      Checks if the given array contains the element to search.

      static <T> T getFirst(T[] array)
      Returns the first element from the given array.

      Returns the following element if available from the array.

      Returns the last element from the given array.

      Returns the previous element if available from the array.

      Returns the previous element if available from the array.

      Returns the first index in the given array that contains the element to search.

    • Constructor Detail

      • ArrayUtil

        private ArrayUtil()
        Forbid instances by this private constructor.

    • Method Detail

      • search

        public static <T> T search(T[] array,
                           IFilter<T> filter)
        Searches an element in the given Iterable instance.
        Parameters:
        array - The instance to search in.
        filter - The filter to select an element.
        Returns:
        The found element or null if no element was found.

      • addAll

        public static <T> T[] addAll(T[] array,
                             T[] toAdd)

        Adds the given elements to the existing array. The result is a new array that contains the other elements in the end.

        Attention: It is not possible to use this method with two null parameters. In this case is an IllegalArgumentException thrown.

        Parameters:
        array - The array to add to.
        toAdd - The elements to add.
        Returns:
        The new created array.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - Both parameters are null.

      • addAll

        public static <T> T[] addAll(T[] array,
                             T[] toAdd,
                             Class<?> newArrayType)

        Adds the given elements to the existing array. The result is a new array that contains the other elements in the end.

        Attention: It is not possible to use this method with two null parameters. In this case is an IllegalArgumentException thrown.

        Parameters:
        array - The array to add to.
        toAdd - The elements to add.
        newArrayType - The type of the new array.
        Returns:
        The new created array.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - Both parameters are null.

      • add

        public static <T> T[] add(T[] array,
                          T toAdd)

        Adds the given element to the existing array. The result is a new array that contains one more element.

        Attention: It is not possible to use this method with two null parameters. In this case is an IllegalArgumentException thrown.

        Parameters:
        array - The array to extend.
        toAdd - The element to add.
        Returns:
        The new created array with one more element.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - Both parameters are null.

      • add

        public static int[] add(int[] array,
                        int toAdd)

        Adds the given element to the existing array. The result is a new array that contains one more element.

        Parameters:
        array - The array to extend.
        toAdd - The element to add.
        Returns:
        The new created array with one more element.

      • insert

        public static <T> T[] insert(T[] array,
                             T toInsert,
                             int index)

        Inserts the given element at the given index to the existing array. The result is a new array that contains one more element.

        Parameters:
        array - The array to extend.
        toInsert - The element to insert.
        index - The index to insert the element at.
        Returns:
        The new created array with one more element.

      • contains

        public static <T> boolean contains(T[] array,
                                   T toSearch)
        Checks if the given array contains the element to search.
        Type Parameters:
        T - The type of the array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array.
        toSearch - The element to search.
        Returns:
        true if the array contains the element or false if not or if the array is null.

      • contains

        public static <T> boolean contains(T[] array,
                                   T toSearch,
                                   Comparator<T> comparator)
        Checks if the given array contains the element to search. The equality is computed via the comparator. Objects are equal if the comparison result is 0.
        Type Parameters:
        T - The type of the array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array.
        toSearch - The element to search.
        comparator - the Comparator to use.
        Returns:
        true if the array contains the element or false if not or if the array is null.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - If the comparator is null.

      • indexOf

        public static <T> int indexOf(T[] array,
                              T toSearch)
        Returns the first index in the given array that contains the element to search.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to search in.
        toSearch - The element to search.
        Returns:
        The first index in the array that contains the element to search or -1 if the elment is not containd in the array.

      • indexOf

        public static <T> int indexOf(T[] array,
                              T toSearch,
                              Comparator<T> comparator)
        Returns the first index in the given array that contains the element to search. The equality is computed via the comparator. Objects are equal if the comparison result is 0.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to search in.
        toSearch - The element to search.
        comparator - the Comparator to use.
        Returns:
        The first index in the array that contains the element to search or -1 if the elment is not containd in the array.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - If the comparator is null.

      • remove

        public static <T> T[] remove(T[] array,
                             T toRemove)
        Removes all occurrences from toRemove in the array. The equality is computed via ObjectUtil.equals(Object, Object).
        Parameters:
        array - The array to remove from.
        toRemove - The element to remove.
        Returns:
        A copy of the array without the element toRemove or null if the given array was null.

      • remove

        public static <T> T[] remove(T[] array,
                             T toRemove,
                             Comparator<T> comparator)
        Removes all occurrences from toRemove in the array. The equality is computed via the comparator. Objects are equal if the comparison result is 0.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to remove from.
        toRemove - The element to remove.
        comparator - The Comparator to use.
        Returns:
        A copy of the array without the element toRemove or null if the given array was null.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - If the comparator is null.

      • toString

        public static <T> String toString(T... array)
        Converts the given array into a String. For each element is Object.toString() used to convert it into a String.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to convert.
        Returns:
        The array as String.

      • toString

        public static <T> String toString(T[] array,
                                  String separator)
        Converts the given array into a String. For each element is Object.toString() used to convert it into a String.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to convert.
        separator - The separator between to array elements.
        Returns:
        The array as String.

      • toString

        public static String toString(int[] array)
        Converts the given array into a String.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to convert.
        Returns:
        The array as String.

      • toString

        public static String toString(int[] array,
                              String separator)
        Converts the given array into a String.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to convert.
        separator - The separator between to array elements.
        Returns:
        The array as String.

      • isEmpty

        public static <T> boolean isEmpty(T[] array)
        Checks if the given array is empty.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to check.
        Returns:
        true array is empty or null, false array is not empty.

      • getPrevious

        public static <T> T getPrevious(T[] array,
                                T toSearch)
        Returns the previous element if available from the array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to search in.
        toSearch - The element for that the previous one is needed.
        Returns:
        The previous element or null if no element was found.

      • getPrevious

        public static <T> T getPrevious(T[] array,
                                T toSearch,
                                Comparator<T> comparator)
                         throws IllegalArgumentException
        Returns the previous element if available from the array. The equality is computed via the comparator. Objects are equal if the comparison result is 0.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to search in.
        toSearch - The element for that the previous one is needed.
        comparator - the Comparator to use.
        Returns:
        The previous element or null if no element was found.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - If the comparator is null.

      • getFollowing

        public static <T> T getFollowing(T[] array,
                                 T toSearch)
        Returns the following element if available from the array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to search in.
        toSearch - The element for that the following one is needed.
        Returns:
        The following element or null if no element was found.

      • getFollowing

        public static <T> T getFollowing(T[] array,
                                 T toSearch,
                                 Comparator<T> comparator)
                          throws IllegalArgumentException
        Returns the following element if available from the array. The equality is computed via the comparator. Objects are equal if the comparison result is 0.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to search in.
        toSearch - The element for that the following one is needed.
        comparator - the Comparator to use.
        Returns:
        The following element or null if no element was found.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - If the comparator is null.

      • isLast

        public static <T> boolean isLast(T[] array,
                                 T toSearch)
        Checks if the given element is the last element in the array.
        Type Parameters:
        T - The type of the array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array.
        toSearch - The element to search.
        Returns:
        true is last element, false is not last element or even not contained in the array.

      • isLast

        public static <T> boolean isLast(T[] array,
                                 T toSearch,
                                 Comparator<T> comparator)
        Checks if the given element is the last element in the array. Objects are equal if the comparison result is 0.
        Type Parameters:
        T - The type of the array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array.
        toSearch - The element to search.
        comparator - the Comparator to use.
        Returns:
        true is last element, false is not last element or even not contained in the array.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - If the comparator is null.

        public static <T> boolean isFirst(T[] array,
                                  T toSearch)
        Checks if the given element is the first element in the array.
        Type Parameters:
        T - The type of the array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array.
        toSearch - The element to search.
        Returns:
        true is first element, false is not first element or even not contained in the array.

      • isFirst

        public static <T> boolean isFirst(T[] array,
                                  T toSearch,
                                  Comparator<T> comparator)
        Checks if the given element is the first element in the array. Objects are equal if the comparison result is 0.
        Type Parameters:
        T - The type of the array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array.
        toSearch - The element to search.
        comparator - the Comparator to use.
        Returns:
        true is first element, false is not first element or even not contained in the array.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - If the comparator is null.

      • getFirst

        public static <T> T getFirst(T[] array)
        Returns the first element from the given array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to get first element from.
        Returns:
        The first element or null if no element is available.

      • getLast

        public static <T> T getLast(T[] array)
        Returns the last element from the given array.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to get last element from.
        Returns:
        The first element or null if no element is available.

      • generatePermutations

        public static <T> T[][] generatePermutations(T[] array)
        Computes all permutations of the given array using the 'Heap's algorithm'.
        Parameters:
        array - The array to generate its permutations.
        Returns:
        The generated permutations or null if the given array is null.
        See Also:
        https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heap's_algorithm

      • generatePermutations

        private static <T> int generatePermutations(T[] array,
                                            int n,
                                            T[][] permutations,
                                            int permutationsIndex)
        Recursive implementation of the 'Heap's algorithm': 
         procedure generate(n : integer, A : array of any):
    if n = 1 then
       output(A)
    else
       for i := 0; i < n; i += 1 do
          generate(n - 1, A)
          if n is even then
             swap(A[i], A[n-1])
          else
             swap(A[0], A[n-1])
          end if
       end for
    end if
        Parameters:
        array - The array to generate its permutations.
        n - The recursive termination criterion.
        permutations - The result List to fill.
        Returns:
        The next index in permutations to fill.
        See Also:
        https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heap's_algorithm
